description: Hand-written seed fixture proving the memory-retrieval eval plumbing. Eight consolidated memories from a hobby-electronics support channel, four operator-labeled queries. Real fixtures accumulate during Phase 1.

conversation.content: My weather station keeps dropping off wifi after a few hours. It's the ESP32 build we talked about.
Last time we saw that, it was the power-save mode on the wifi driver. Did you set WIFI_PS_NONE?
That fixed it! Also FYI I moved the rain gauge to the north fence, readings look saner now.

memories.summary: kestrel's weather station is an ESP32 build; it was dropping off wifi after a few hours until wifi power-save was disabled (WIFI_PS_NONE), which fixed it.
kestrel moved the rain gauge to the north fence, which made its readings saner (previous location caused splash-over anomalies).
kestrel prefers PlatformIO over the Arduino IDE and keeps all firmware projects in a monorepo called birdhouse.
The greenhouse humidity sensor (SHT31) reads about 4% high; agreed to apply a -4 offset in firmware rather than replace it.
robin's soldering iron is a Pinecil; the USB-C supply must be 20V-capable or the tip won't reach temperature.
The shared parts bin has run out of BC547 transistors; robin volunteered to reorder from the usual distributor.
kestrel's solar charge controller logs to MQTT topic garden/power; retention on the broker is 7 days.
Decision: all outdoor sensor enclosures get conformal coating after the spring condensation failures.

queries.text: Why did the ESP32 weather station keep losing its wifi connection?
queries.note: m1 is the only memory about the wifi dropout and its power-save root cause.

queries.text: What do we know about kestrel's rain gauge placement?
queries.note: m2 records the move to the north fence and why; m1 is the same project but does not answer placement.

queries.text: Is there anything wrong with the humidity readings in the greenhouse?
queries.note: m4 records the SHT31's +4% bias and the firmware-offset decision.

queries.text: What power-related gotchas have come up with kestrel's outdoor projects?
queries.note: Broad query with two labeled hits: wifi power-save on the weather station (m1) and the solar charge controller MQTT logging (m7). m5 is power-related but robin's, not kestrel's.
